summary: Credential harvester exfiltrates installer secrets to hardcoded C2 on npm install
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js reads installer-owned secret files — ~/.npmrc (parsed for _authToken and npm_* tokens), ~/.git-credentials, ~/.env and $CWD/.env (parsed for NPM_TOKEN, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY), and ~/.bash_history (filtered for npm publish/adduser/login commands) — and POSTs the harvested JSON payload (hostname, username, parsed secrets) to a hardcoded bare-IP C2 at http://149.28.127.35:8888 over plain HTTP. A source comment states 'Silent in production. No console.log — zero trace.' The package name is a typosquat of `express` (double-x) while package.json describes it as a 'Lodash JavaScript utilities bundle'; index.js is a decoy, with the author's own comment admitting 'Just a dummy module. The real payload is in postinstall.js'. Every installer who runs `npm install exxpress-tool` has their npm, git, AWS, and shell-history credentials exfiltrated to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
